entry.title: Need to remove trailing Zeros (after decimal) from the work duration in PM order operation data
entry.content:
Members  ,

In Maintenance work order operation tab (Fiori app) app, the duration is converted to decimal value with trailing zeros automatically by system. we need not to have this. Requirement is to see the value (without decimal) only unless the decimal value is specified in a field specifically by the user. System must always show without decimal value in all the apps for maintenance order operations

Currently it's showing it differently for different FIORI apps. Please let me know how to fix this.

entry.title: Re: Production Order Settlement with TECO/DLV Status
entry.content: The final settlement after the variance calculation can only happen when the order status is TECO/DLV. The Order status for the period 12/2024 is not TECO/DLV. WIP is calculated & settled only for the period 12/2024 and Variance is calculated & settled only for the period 01/2025. Hence the final settlement after the variance calculation can only happen for the period 01/2025 during which the order status is TECO.

entry.title: This new Data Architecture called "Shift Left" is a game changer and the industry is taking a turn
entry.content:
Before I set ground, let me talk about the two big blocks in Data Analytics of modern ages, Data Streaming and Data Lakes or LakeHouses

image by Author

 

 

The Lakehouse is a relatively new concept that merges the capabilities of a data lake and a data warehouse. Meanwhile, data streaming serves as the foundation for real-time processing and ensures data consistency between real-time and non-real-time systems.

A Lakehouse is typically used for analytical use cases, whereas data streaming supports both operational and analytical applications, bridging the gap between them.

 

Data streaming is built on event-driven architecture, a concept that has existed for over 20 years with message brokers. However, data streaming introduces fundamental differences. I usually describe it using four pillars:

  1. Real-Time Messaging

    • Similar to traditional message brokers but built to scale massively—processing millions of messages per second.
    • Supports transactional workloads with features like exactly-once processing, which is crucial for applications like payment processing.

  2. Persistence Layer

    • Unlike traditional message brokers, data streaming persists events.
    • This allows businesses to define retention policies—from a few hours (for logs or IoT data) to months or years (for critical business data).
    • This prevents the need to repeatedly query the ERP systems.

  3. Data Integration

    • Unlike message brokers, which require additional ETL tools, data streaming platforms offer built-in integration.
    • They connect monolith systems (relational databases, ERPs, mainframes) and cloud-native services (object stores, cloud Lakehouses, SaaS applications).

  4. Data Processing

    • Stream processing enables continuous transformation and enrichment of data.
    • Instead of storing and analyzing raw data later, it can be aggregated, filtered, and enriched before reaching its destination.
    • Used for fraud detection, predictive maintenance, customer recommendations, and even real-time AI inference.

Data Streaming Strengths:

  • Best for processing data in motion with low-latency event-driven architectures.
  • Used in real-time analytics, fraud detection, monitoring, alerting, and transactional processing.
  • Core banking systems, ERP solutions, and AI inference engines often use Kafka and Flink under the hood.
  • Ideal for continuous AI model inference where immediate action is required.

Lakehouse Strengths:

  • Originates from data warehouse and data lake concepts (e.g., Snowflake, Delta Lake).
  • Best for batch processing, historical data analysis, and business intelligence.
  • Used for long-term data storage, reporting, and model training.
  • AI workloads like model training and large-scale batch inference fit well in a Lakehouse.

Rather than choosing one over the other, the best strategy is to combine them:

  • Data streaming processes data in motion, enabling real-time decision-making.
  • Lakehouses store processed data at rest, making it available for historical analysis and AI training.
  • Streaming data can be persisted in a Lakehouse for further analytics.

Nevertheless, the key it results in using open table formats, with Apache Iceberg, we can store data once and query it with any tool and this is what drives this blog.

For this, a new Data Processing architecture called Shift Left has emerged as a response to inefficiencies in earlier data processing paradigms Medallion or ETL. The main problem of the Medallion and ETL architectures is its inefficient and has an elevated compute cost because data is copied over and over again, and every time.

In Multi-Hop Medallion Architecture, raw data ("bronze") undergoes iterative refinement through silver (cleaned) and gold (enriched) layers via batch ETL processes. This required duplicative processing at each layer, increasing compute costs, latency between data generation and availability and silos where operational/analytical systems used different datasets.

The Multi-Hop Architecture
Data is typically extracted, transformed, and loaded (ETL) into an analytics environment following a multi-hop process:

  1. Bronze Layer (Landing Zone) – Raw data from operational systems, including Kafka topics, SaaS endpoints, or text files, is ingested here.
  2. Silver Layer (Refined Data) – Data is cleaned, structured, and enriched, making it usable for business purposes.
  3. Gold Layer (Business-Ready Data) – Purpose-built datasets are created for specific applications, reports, or exports.

Medallion architecture by Databricks

 

This Medallion Architecture (Bronze → Silver → Gold) is widely used but has significant drawbacks.

Challenges with Multi-Hop Architectures

  1. Slow Data Processing

    • Most multi-hop architectures rely on scheduled batch processing.
    • Each transformation step introduces a delay (e.g., a 15-minute interval per hop), resulting in outdated insights.

  2. High Costs

    • Every hop requires additional storage and processing power, increasing infrastructure expenses.

  3. Brittle Pipelines

    • Different teams manage different layers (e.g., database engineers, data engineers, and analysts), leading to coordination challenges and frequent breakages.

  4. Duplicate Pipelines

    • To avoid disruptions, teams often create their own redundant data pipelines, adding unnecessary complexity.

  5. Similar Yet Different Datasets

    • Multiple versions of the same dataset emerge, leading to confusion about which one is the authoritative source.
    • Inconsistent data can erode trust, especially when reports show conflicting numbers.

 

Shift Left addresses these inefficiencies by moving data preparation earlier in the process.

Shift Left Architecture. By Author

 

 

The key principles include:

  1. A Stream-First Approach

    • Instead of periodic batch jobs, data is processed in real-time using event-driven architectures (EDAs).
    • Technologies like Kafka and Change Data Capture (CDC) connectors ensure that updates are immediately available.

  2. Stream-to-Table Conversion with Amazon S3

    • Amazon now automatically converts streams into Apache Iceberg tables, making structured data instantly accessible.
    • Iceberg tables can be read by multiple engines (e.g., Trino, Flink, Presto, Spark, Databricks, and Snowflake).

  3. Integration with Existing Analytics Workflows

    • Shift Left doesn’t replace existing architectures but enhances them.
    • Cleaned and structured data from streams seamlessly integrates into the Silver layer, enabling faster and more reliable insights.

Benefits of Shift Left

  • Faster Data Availability – Real-time streaming eliminates batch processing delays.
  • Lower Costs – Fewer data copies reduce storage and processing expenses.
  • Improved Reliability – Stronger schema enforcement prevents pipeline breakages.
  • Simpler Architecture – Eliminates redundant pipelines and conflicting datasets.
  • Incremental Adoption – Companies can gradually migrate workloads to Shift Left without disrupting operations.

 

The Shift Left Architecture builds on the concept introduced by McKinsey consultants called data products, which are central to modern **data mesh** principles. The goal is to unify operational and  analytical workloads by creating high-quality, consistent, and real-time data products.

source; McKinsey "a-better-way-to-put-your-data-to-work"

 

The Shift Left Approach addresses the medallion inefficiencies by moving data processing closer to its source—on the left side of the architecture. At its core, this approach relies on event-driven data streaming for real-time, scalable, and reliable processing.

The concept is to create the data product once, and that will be available for multiple systems to consume. This is cheaper, faster and supports both Operational and Analytical use cases with Iceberg format and ACID transactions guarantee.

Amazon S3 with its Iceberg capability, as a table format to unify operational and analytical workloads, allows businesses to store data once (e.g., in an object store Amazon S3) and consume it across various platforms (e.g., Snowflake, Athena) without requiring additional processing or connectors, but other cloud Table formats like Delta Lake could be served.

entry.title: Re: How to retrieve context of multiple instances in 1 Pa...
entry.content:
As far as I know there is no flow function to retrieve contexts and there is no API to retrieve all contexts from a subset of process instances.

So you can create a logic flow like this:

FIrst, I create an integration to the destination you had to create for all the trigger/process flow functions.

Then you create the above logic flow:

  1. I created a second page variable (loop variable) to hold all the instances, and copied all the info I retrieved into it.
  2. I used the new HTTP Destination Request flow function:
    • Set the destination to the one we added above.
    • Method GET
    • Path is a formula like this: "/public/workflow/rest/v1/workflow-instances/" + pageVars.PurchaseOrderInstancesLoop[0].id + "/context"
  3. I created a simple list of objects page variable called Contexts, and here I set it to the following formula: WITH_ITEM(pageVars.Contexts, outputs["HTTP destination request"].resBodyParsed)
  4. I removed the first item in my loop variable, by setting it to this: REMOVE_ITEMS_AT(pageVars.PurchaseOrderInstancesLoop,0) 
  5. I check if the loop variable is empty. 
    • If not, we loop back.
    • If yes, we end the loop and here I just display the Contexts variable.

Hope that helps, and hope I understood what you wanted.

entry.title: FIORI Elements Field Highlighting not working in CREATE Mode but EDIT Mode
entry.content:
Hi, 

i have generated FIORI Elements List Report Page. As Backend i am using BOPF with SAP NW 7.50.

UI5 Version is 1.108.26

Problem:

Field highlighting only works in EDIT Mode, in CREATE Mode there is no error Message at all. Removing Attribute Parameter leads to error message displayed but without reference to Field.

  METHOD /bobf/if_frw_validation~execute.
    CLEAR: eo_message, et_failed_key.

    DATA: it_example_data TYPE z_t_pmc_scen_auth,
          location        TYPE /bobf/s_frw_location,
          lo_cm           TYPE REF TO /bobf/cm_frw.

    io_read->retrieve(
      EXPORTING
        iv_node                 = zif_pm_c_scen_auth_c=>sc_node-zpm_c_scen_auth
        it_key                  = it_key
      IMPORTING
        eo_message              = eo_message
        et_data                 = it_example_data
        et_failed_key           = et_failed_key
    ).

    IF eo_message IS NOT BOUND.
      eo_message = /bobf/cl_frw_factory=>get_message(  ).
    ENDIF.

    LOOP AT it_example_data INTO DATA(line).

      "Error occured
      eo_message->add_message(
        EXPORTING
          is_msg       = VALUE #( msgid = 'ZCO' msgno = '002' msgty = 'E' )
          iv_node      = is_ctx-node_key
          iv_key       = line-key
          iv_attribute = zif_pm_c_scen_auth_c=>sc_node_attribute-zif_pm_c_scen_auth-auth_role
*      iv_lifetime  =

      ).
      APPEND VALUE #( key = line-key ) TO et_failed_key.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.

Result in EDIT Mode:

Result in Create Mode with Attribute provided: SAVE is prevented but there is no error message at all.
Removing Parameter Attribute:

Is this a problem with used SAPUI5 Version?

entry.title: BAPI_MATERIAL_SAVEREPLICA | Z-fields cannot be updated
entry.content:
Dear Dev community

I am in an S/4HANA system (standard). I am trying to change a Z-field in the MARA table using the BAPI BAPI_MATERIAL_SAVEREPLICA. To do this, I have added the Z field to MARA and the enhancement structures BAPI_TE_E1MARA2 and BAPI_TE_E1MARA2X (here ZZMARKE). I also take into account the long material numbers (MATERIAL_LONG). The Z field is not used in the dialog (e.g. Fiori) and was therefore created as a classic Z-field (append).

See the following program.

report zkwe_material_create.

parameters: p_matnr  type matnr.

data:
  ls_return      type bapie1ret2,
  lt_return      type table of bapie1ret2,
  ls_headdata    type bapie1matheader,
  lt_headdata    type table of bapie1matheader,
  ls_clientdata  type bapie1mara,
  ls_clientdatax type bapie1marax,
  lt_clientdata  type table of bapie1mara,
  lt_clientdatax type table of bapie1marax,
  ls_description type bapie1makt,
  lt_description type table of bapie1makt,
  ls_extension   type bapie1parex,
  ls_extensionx  type bapie1parexx,
  lt_extension   type table of bapie1parex,
  lt_extensionx  type table of bapie1parexx,
  ls_ext_mara    type bapi_te_e1mara2,
  ls_ext_marax   type bapi_te_e1mara2x.

" General data needed for product creation
ls_headdata-material_long       = p_matnr.
ls_headdata-ind_sector     = 'O'.
ls_headdata-matl_type      = 'HAWA'.
ls_headdata-basic_view     = 'X'.

insert ls_headdata into table lt_headdata .

* Client data:
ls_clientdata-material_long = ls_headdata-material_long .
ls_clientdata-base_uom      = 'ST' .
insert ls_clientdata into table lt_clientdata .

* Client data x-fields:
ls_clientdatax-material_long = ls_headdata-material_long .
ls_clientdatax-base_uom      = 'X' .
insert ls_clientdatax into table lt_clientdatax .

* Description:
ls_description-material_long = ls_headdata-material_long .
ls_description-langu         = 'DE' .
ls_description-matl_desc     = 'Hello world!' .
insert ls_description into table lt_description .

* Extensions:
ls_ext_mara-material    = ls_headdata-material_long.
ls_ext_mara-zzmarke     = 'Content'.
ls_extension-material   = ls_headdata-material.
ls_extension-structure  = 'BAPI_TE_E1MARA2'.
ls_extension-valuepart1 = ls_ext_mara.
insert ls_extension into table lt_extension.

ls_ext_marax-material    = ls_headdata-material_long.
ls_ext_marax-zzmarke     = abap_true.
ls_extensionx-material   = ls_headdata-material_long.
ls_extensionx-structure  = 'BAPI_TE_E1MARA2X'.
ls_extensionx-valuepart1 = ls_ext_marax.
insert ls_extensionx into table lt_extensionx.

call function 'BAPI_MATERIAL_SAVEREPLICA'
  exporting
    noappllog           = abap_false
    nochangedoc         = abap_false
    testrun             = abap_false
    inpfldcheck         = abap_false
  importing
    return              = ls_return
  tables
    headdata            = lt_headdata
    clientdata          = lt_clientdata
    clientdatax         = lt_clientdatax
    materialdescription = lt_description
    extensionin         = lt_extension
    extensioninx        = lt_extensionx
    returnmessages      = lt_return.

commit work.

write: / ls_return-message, ls_return-message_v1.
loop at lt_return into ls_return.
  write: / ls_return-message, ls_return-message_v1.
endloop.

The program correctly creates or changes the material, but the Z field is not transferred.

Is there anything special to consider in the S/4HANA context?

Thank you very much in advance for your support.

Best regards, Kurt

entry.title: Handling Dynamic Non-Persistent CDS Entities in SAP CAP
entry.content:
Scenario 1: Reading Entity with On-Read Event

Reading a non-persistent CDS entity dynamically is straightforward. However, retrieving data based on different OData query parameters requires careful handling.

For example, consider the following request to retrieve department details for an employee:

 

/odata/v4/EmployeeService/Employee(12345)/employeeDepartments

 

To extract the ID from Employee, use:

 

String employeeId = (String)CqnAnalyzer.create(context.getModel()).analyze(context.getCqn().ref()).rootKeys().get("id");

 

Scenario 2: Reading Entity Directly

If an entity is requested directly, such as:

 

/odata/v4/EmployeeService/EmployeeDepartment(67890)

 

Then, the approach to extracting the ID changes:

 

String departmentId = (String)CqnAnalyzer.create(context.getModel()).analyze(context.getCqn().ref()).targetKeyValues().get("ID");

 

Scenario 3: Handling Filters in Requests

When handling filters, we need to extract conditions dynamically and create a map for further processing. Consider this request:

 

/odata/v4/EmployeeService/EmployeeDepartment?$filter=departmentType eq 'IT' and employeeID eq '12345'

 

To parse the filter conditions:

 

Optional<CqnPredicate> whereClause = readEventContext.getCqn().where();
Map<String, String> whereMap = new HashMap<>();
final String[] currentField = { null };

if (whereClause.isPresent()) {
    whereClause.get().tokens().forEach(token -> {
        if (token instanceof ElementRefImpl<?>) {
            currentField[0] = String.valueOf(((ElementRefImpl<?>) token).targetSegment());
        } else if (token instanceof CqnStrLiteral) {
            String value = ((CqnStrLiteral) token).value();
            if (currentField[0] != null) {
                whereMap.put(currentField[0], value);
                currentField[0] = null;
            }
        }
    });
}
System.out.println(whereMap);

 

Scenario 4: Handling Count Parameter

If $count=true is passed in the request, the result cannot be a simple list. An exception occurs such as "The query result misses a value for $count".

Instead, we must use ResultBuilder to handle the inline count properly. If not handled correctly, the query result will miss a value for $count, which is a common mistake when using readEventContext.setResult(list).

Example request:

 

/odata/v4/EmployeeService/EmployeeDepartment?$count=true

 

To process this in code:

 

ResultBuilder resultBuilder = ResultImpl.selectedRows(dataList);
if (readEventContext.getCqn().hasInlineCount()) {
    resultBuilder.inlineCount(paginatedList.size());
}
readEventContext.setResult(resultBuilder.result());

 

Conclusion

Handling dynamic non-persistent CDS entities in SAP CAP requires carefully extracting and processing different query parameters. By implementing these techniques, we can ensure a seamless experience similar to persistent OData entities, improving performance and usability.

entry.title: Re: Intelligent Invoice Scanning
entry.content:
Hi Stefan 

Many thanks for this very helpful update. 

May I ask for supplier invoices, can SAP only scan the non-items information ( like invoice date, invoice number, subtotal amount, tax amount and total invoice value etc), and skip the step of scanning the Items section. 

Because our supplier invoices generally have over 100 line items of same nature and we only need one line (being the total amount to be entered into General Ledger account).  

For example, our transport supplier invoices have over 100 deliveries on the invoices for the past week, yet Accounts payable officer only needs to enter the total charge of the 100 deliveries under Outbound Freight General ledger when processing the invoice.

Currently, if we use SAP's intelligent scanning feature, we had to remove all the 100 deliveries item lines auto-scanned and then create an invoice.  

Is there a way to avoid the need of deleting all the invoiced items please? Many thanks. 

Best regards

Kathy

entry.title: Access for users to update allocation rules in SAC
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a requirement from business for allocation setup in SAP Analytics Cloud. Users want to do the allocation for fixed cost plan data in SAC using the standard allocation process. In the current application it is handled through universal allocations in S/4. The requirement from business side is that users should be able to update the rules (change source, target, driver filters) in the allocation step in data action as per need for each cycle. As it is a technical change in data action configuration, we understand that users will need developer kind of access with edit permission for data action. Also if there are any changes, that needs to be done in development system and transported to production system. There is some risk associated with this from security side. 

There are 2 concerns:

  1. What access will be needed for users to update allocation rules? Is there any other way without giving developer role?
  2. Is it possible to update rules directly in production system?

Could you please guide us on how to proceed with requirement?

entry.title: Setup Resource Management Capability in SAP Projects and Resource Management
entry.content:
Effective Project and Resource Management (PRM) is essential for delivering successful projects in today’s dynamic business environment. SAP SPRM enables organizations to strategically match skilled resources to project demands, optimizing resource utilization and aligning with business goals. 

 

Key Features 

  1. Resource Profiles

    • Enables resources to create profiles showcasing their skills, experience, and availability, simplifying staffing decisions for resource managers. 

  2. Project Data Integration 

    • Facilitates real-time replication of project demands from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for efficient staffing. 

  3. Centralized Request Management 

    • Manage multiple project resource requests in a single place, improving transparency and efficiency. 

  4. Advanced Resource Staffing 

    • Offers skill-based matching, availability checks, and resource utilization tracking. 

  5. Boost Resource Utilization 

    • Minimizes idle time and maximizes productivity by aligning resources with project demands. 

 

 

Licensing Information 

Resource Management Capability in SAP Project and Resource Managment is a separately licensed scope item in addition to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition.  

  1. Pricing: 

    • Based on Full Use Equivalent (FUE) per month. 

    • Pro-rated monthly pricing for contracts shorter than a year. 

  2. Payment Methods: 

    • Invoice (all countries) or credit card (region-specific). 

  3. Supported Languages: 

    • Includes English, Chinese, French, German, Japanese, and more. 

  4. Flexible Licensing: 

    • Scalable for businesses of all sizes, from small teams to multi-regional enterprises. 

For more details, visit SAP RM Page. 

 

 

Integration Prerequisites 

To facilitate smooth communication and data exchange, the following prerequisites should be met.

  • Mandatory: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition for project demand replication. 
  • Optional: Integration with SAP SuccessFactors for talent profiles and workforce capability. 
    • Customers can leverage either SAP SuccessFactors or SAP S/4HANA Cloud for talent management. 

 

 

Scope Item Activation 

Activate the following to enable project replication and resource management: 

  • J11: Customer Project Management - Project-Based Services. 
  • 1A8: Internal Project Management - Project-Based Services. 
  • 6JO: Resource Management for Projects. 
  • J54: Overhead Cost Accounting (for cost center replication). 

Verification Steps: 

  1. Manage Your Solution App: View solution scope via SAP Fiori. 

     

  2. SAP Central Business Configuration: In the Scope and Organizational Structure phase, navigate to the Activities tab. Search for Define Scope. 

Helpful SAP Notes and KBAs: 

  • SAP Note 2819010: How to check if a scope item is activated in the system. 
  • SAP Note 3045179: Request to activate scope items. 

 

Subscription to Resource Management Capability 

To effectively subscribe to the Resource Management Capability, follow these steps outlined to ensure a successful subscription:

Steps to subscribe: 

  1. Access SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) cockpit. 
  2. Navigate to the Service Marketplace and search for "Resource Management".
  3. Select plan as Standard, subscribe, and launch the app. 

 

 

Guided Workflow for RM Setup 

The resource management application requires integration with SAP cloud solutions to replicate and exchange data, leading to several manual steps. These steps have been combined into a guided workflow, delivered through the Cloud Integration Automation Service (CIAS).

The Cloud Integration Automation Service  offers an automated workflow for integration. 

Steps: 

  1. Log into SAP for Me → Systems & Provisioning dashboard -> -> Overview tab, locate the Landscape Applications card -> Maintenance Planner. 
  2. Use the Maintenance Planner to plan integration. On the home page of Maintenance Planner, choose Plan for Cloud Integration Scenario. 
  3. Search for "Resource Management capability in SAP Project and Resource Management(6JO)" and choose tenants for integration. 
  4. Select a tenant for each of the systems you want to integrate and choose Generate Workflow. 

Note: In case you are not onboarded to enhanced maintenance planner, then Plan for Cloud Integration may not be available. Please follow the SAP KBA 2921927 to get it resolved. 

 

 

Set Up the Communication in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for Project Replication

To successfully replicate project data from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, it is essential to configure the communication settings accurately.  

Communication Scenario 

Direction 

Related Scope Item 

Resource Management in SAP Project and Resource Management - API Integration (SAP_COM_0A11) 

Inbound 

Resource Management for Projects (6JO) 

Resource Management in SAP Project and Resource Management - UI Integration (SAP_COM_0840) 

Outbound 

Resource Management for Projects (6JO) 

 

Step 1: Create Communication Users 

  1. Access the System 

    1. Log in to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system with an authorized user account that possesses the SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR role. 

  2. Maintain Communication Users 

    1. Open the Maintain Communication Users application. 

    2. Create a new user with the following details: 

      • Username: Enter a unique username. 

      • Description: Provide a descriptive label for the user. 

  3. Select Authentication Method

    1. Password-Based Authentication: 

      • Input a secure password or use the Propose Password option. 

      • Note: Passwords must be a minimum of 20 characters and include at least one special character. 

    2. Certificate-Based Authentication: 

      • Upload an X.509 client certificate signed by an authorized certification authority. 

      • Refer to SAP Note 2801396 for details on approved certification authorities. 

    3. Recommendation: For enhanced security, use certificate-based authentication. 

  4. Save the User Configuration 

 

Step 2: Create Communication Systems 

  1. For Data Exchange with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: 

    1. System ID: e.g., S4HANA. 

    2. System Name: Provide a meaningful name. 

    3. Host Name: Enter the host name of your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system (e.g., myxxxxxx.s4hana.ondemand.com or myxxxxxx.s4hana.cloud.sap). Replace <myxxxxxx> with your system’s specific host name. 

    4. Inbound Communication User: Assign the user created in the previous step. 

  2. For Navigation from Launchpad to Resource Management: 

    1. System ID: e.g., RM. 

    2. System Name: Provide a descriptive name. 

    3. Host Name: Input the subscription URL for the Resource Management application on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). 

Step 3: Create Communication Arrangements 

  1. API Integration Finalization with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: 

    1. Open the Communication Arrangements application and select New. 

    2. Scenario: Choose SAP_COM_0A11 (Resource Management - API Integration). 

    3. Arrangement Name: Input a unique name. 

    4. Assign the communication system created for data exchange in Step 2. 

    5. Note: The username will populate automatically. Save the arrangement. 


  2. UI Integration Setup for Resource Management Navigation: 

    1. Open the Communication Arrangements application and select New. 

    2. Scenario: Choose SAP_COM_0840 (Resource Management - UI Integration). 

    3. Arrangement Name: Input a unique name. 

    4. Assign the communication system created for launchpad navigation in Step 2. 

    5. Save the arrangement. 

 

 

Integration with SAP Cloud ALM 

SAP RM integrates with SAP Cloud ALM for monitoring and managing master/transactional data replication. 

Steps: 

  1. Verify that the resource management service is listed in the Landscape Management app of SAP Cloud ALM. 
  2. Activate monitoring data collection. Follow the instructions in the topic Activate the Monitoring Data Collection on the page Activate the Monitoring Data Collection for Integration in  SAP Project and Resource Management, resource management capability. Other sections such as Customize the Monitoring Data Collection are optional. 

Refer to the SAP Cloud ALM setup guide for additional configuration details. 

 

 

User Management  

To configure user management for resource management, you need to define roles and assign role collections to users. 

Note: When you register your subaccount in your identity provider, you must ensure that the user attribute E-Mail has the assertion attribute mail. This mapping is essential for the use of the My Project Experience app. 

 

Role Template 

Description 

Available Attributes 

Tiles on SAP Fiori Launchpad 

RequesterSuperuser 

Users can create and manage resource requests for all processing resource organizations. 

Assign this super user role to the existing role collection that authorizes users to create and manage resource requests for all requested resource organizations. 

Not applicable 

Manage Resource Requests 

ResourceManagerSuperuser 

Users can staff all resource requests and view resource utilization. 

Assign this super user role to the existing role collection that authorizes users to staff resource requests and view resource utilization for all processing resource organizations. 

Not applicable 

Staff Resource Requests 

Manage Resource Utilization 

My Resources 

Requester 

Users can create and manage resource requests for authorized resource organizations. 

RequestedResourceOrganization 

Manage Resource Requests 

ResourceManager 

Users can staff resource requests and view the resource utilization for authorized resource organizations. 

ProcessingResourceOrganization 

Staff Resource Requests 

Manage Resource Utilization 

My Resources 

ProjectTeamMember 

Users can update their own project experience. 

Not applicable 

My Project Experience 

My Assignments 

ResourceManagementAdministrator 

Users can set the default language for language-dependent master data. 

Not applicable 

Set Default Language 

MyAssignmentEditor 

Users can update their assignments. 

Not applicable 

My Assignments 

 

You have the following options to configure the attributes: 

  • Unrestricted: The role is authorized for all resource organizations. 
    Note: The unrestricted access option for the roles Requester and ResourceManager are not available anymore. Use the super user role templates RequesterSuperuser and ResourceManagerSuperuser instead. 
  • Static: The role is authorized for the resource organizations you specify. Ensure you enter the IDs of the resource organizations, which have 5 characters and can be looked up in the Manage Resource Organizations app. 

Note: Requester and RequesterSuperuser roles should not be assigned together to same users. Same with ResourceManager and  ResourceManagerSuperuser.

entry.title: Re: Access for users to update allocation rules in SAC
entry.content:
@Vivek27 

Allocations Data Actions in SAC are driven by 3 items, Source Dimension, Driver Dimension and Target Dimension. All of these dimension will support parameters which can be provided at execution time. This can help you select each of these items at run time to influence your Allocation run.

Another approach could be if the above does not work to have multiple allocation setup for different rules and the user runs them based on the rule set they want to run.

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

It looks the same as all the other User Statuses but will not accept any of the Status Profiles.

If anyone has any ideas it would be much appreciated

entry.title: Re: How to upload files to DMS using SAP Build Apps and use them in SAP Build Process Automation:Par
entry.content:

How to create the destination of "sap_process_automation_document_store" is specified in the help document. Therefore, I doubt that adding a property for use with SAP Build Apps to this would make it unsupported on the SAP Build Process Automation side.
If this is supported, I think it would be better to combine the destinations into one, as you say.

entry.title: Re: 🔴 Filtering of RAP business events in SAP BTP ABAP Environment and in AEM
entry.content:
Filtering is supported in S4HC you can see it in the video or here as well - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Q4H0LNZi7Dg&list=PL6RpkC85SLQDTp97PAUVi6KYKRHIKtaii&index=2. That said, I believe you are referring to filtering in the event broker, which is handled differently in EMIS/AEM compared to Event Hub.

  • In EMIS/AEM, all the filtering that can be done is via topics, aka topic-based filtering. Therefore, it is important to surface the data at the topic level if you want to do some filtering in EMIS/AEM.
  • In Event Hub this is a bit different because it allows you to do content-based filtering. In this case, you can configure filters based on the data that's available in the CloudEvent header fields.

entry.title: Demystifying Core Concepts in SAP EWM Outbound : Waves, WOCR, Queue & Resource Management
entry.content:
Introduction:

I have often heard from beginners in SAP EWM that they find certain terms in the outbound process confusing. Specifically, they struggle with understanding concepts such as activity area, queue, wave, warehouse order creation rule, and resource in EWM. In this blog, I would like to explain these terms and how they are interconnected in a sequential manner.

Outbound Delivery Order/Warehouse Request:

The sales process begins with the creation of the sales order. The supply planning team in the business then creates the outbound delivery in the S4 system. Once the ERP outbound delivery is created, it is distributed to the EWM system, allowing us to view it in our warehouse monitor.
Each outbound delivery consists of multiple line items, which we refer to as outbound delivery items or warehouse request items. This is because an outbound delivery is also known as a warehouse request. For example, a warehouse request may contain 10 items, meaning there are 10-line items in that outbound delivery.

Wave:

Now, let's briefly discuss the concept of a "wave." In this blog, I won't cover the full concept of waves, but I will provide a brief overview so we can understand the term before diving deeper into the topic.
A wave consists of a set of warehouse request items, which means it is a group of outbound delivery items. As I mentioned earlier, a wave focuses on warehouse request items rather than the header level.

Why is a wave necessary?

Essentially, a wave groups warehouse request items based on certain parameters that we can configure in the system.
For example, if there are 100-line items in a warehouse request across 10 different activity areas from which materials need to be picked, and if we set the wave parameters based on the activity area, the system will create 10 different waves. Each wave will then contain 10-line items, where all items in a wave belong to the same activity area, as this is the configuration we require.
But why are you doing this? Instead of a single warehouse operator having to go through the entire warehouse to pick materials from one corner and then move to another corner to pick up additional materials, we can implement waves.

A wave groups the list of items that need to be picked from a specific area, allowing us to assign that group to the warehouse operator responsible for that area. This way, we can create another wave for a different corner of the warehouse, significantly reducing picking time.

Waves primarily group warehouse items for the picking task. While the activity area is a simple example, we have more complex scenarios where we can group the warehouse request line items based on various criteria such as the ship-to party (customer), shipping route, or transport (carrier), among others.

Do you think waves serve just that purpose? Of course, not; waves have many features, including settings for when we need to release them.
Now, let’s assume a wave is created based on the activity area. What is the next step? The next step is creating the warehouse task. Waves are primarily for grouping delivery items.

We also have a concept called wave release, which allows us to set the system to release the wave automatically or manually. Once the wave is released, the system creates the warehouse task.
Now we can dive deeper into that.

In the above screen, all the warehouse items are assigned to a single wave because they require picking from the same activity area ( Z501).
Now, regarding the wave release: I am going to release wave number 12. When I do this, the system will create warehouse tasks for the items in my wave, which includes 16 warehouse request items. For example, the system may create 16 warehouse tasks during this process.

Warehouse Order Creation Rule:

The system will now look for the warehouse orders to assign to the warehouse tasks. The term "warehouse order" refers to a package that holds the warehouse tasks.

Let’s assume we have 16 warehouse tasks that need warehouse orders assigned to them. (In practice, they are already assigned, but I will explain how they are assigned to the warehouse orders.)

Each warehouse task we created through the wave release is looking for the corresponding warehouse order. EWM introduces a rule for creating warehouse orders for these tasks, known as the "Warehouse Order Creation Rule” (WOCR).

All warehouse tasks then undergo a filtering process governed by the WOCR. To elaborate, we have 16 warehouse tasks, and each task is associated with a warehouse number, an activity area, and an activity. These warehouse tasks will search for the appropriate warehouse order creation rule in the table below.

All 16 warehouse tasks will follow the first line mentioned in the table above and will look for the warehouse order creation rule “WOR1.”

The system checks whether each task meets the criteria for WOR1, and if it does, it selects the appropriate Warehouse Order Creation Rule (WOCR) to create and assign the Warehouse Order.

How Does the System Check for Suitable Rules?
We set the WOCR with multiple parameters. When checking the warehouse tasks, if a task matches the rules defined by the WOCR, it will be accepted, and the WO will be created.

What Are Those Rules? Here’s an Example:
We have two Warehouse Order Creation Rules (WOCR) based on specific parameters:

1. WO Creation Rule 1 (WOR1 - Heavy Items):
This rule is designed for heavy items, as they should be handled with a forklift rather than manually.
-If the total weight of the products in a warehouse task exceeds 10 kg, the task will be routed to WOR1 (this is referred to as the item filter).
-All tasks with a total weight greater than 10 kg will be processed by WOR1 to create the WO and assign the tasks accordingly.
There are limits to consider:
-The total weight of the warehouse order should not exceed 200 kg (the sum of weights of all tasks assigned to that warehouse order).
-A maximum of 10 warehouse tasks can be assigned to a single WO.

2.WO Creation Rule 2 (WOR2 - Lightweight Items):
This rule applies to tasks that weigh less than 10 kg.
-WOR2 follows WOR1 in the priority sequence.
-It applies relevant filters and limits for lighter items.
Based on the above rules, any task under 10 kg will be processed with the creation rule WOR2, which will create a warehouse order and apply the necessary filters and limits.

Please refer to the tables below for more detailed information.Now based on the rule,
Warehouse task, #2249 and #2250 is assigned with warehouse order #5223, why?

For light items weighing 10 kg each, WT# 2249 has a total quantity of 7, resulting in a weight of 84 kg, which exceeds the 10 kg limit. Therefore, it triggers the rule WOR1, and a warehouse order is created.

In addition, there's task# 2250, which has a weight of 56 Kg. This task is included in the same warehouse order since our total WO capacity is 200 kg, and we can have a maximum of 10 tasks per WO.

In total, we have 16 warehouse tasks, but the system only assigned 2 tasks to one warehouse order. This is likely because adding another task would exceed the allowed weight limit of 200 kg for that warehouse order.
Now, will take another example,

From the above screen, it seems that Warehouse Task #2255 is linked to Warehouse Order #5229. Since the total quantity of the task is less than 10 kg, it meets the criteria for the rule WOR2. Therefore, this warehouse task will successfully identify the Warehouse Order creation Rule (WOR2) applicable to it.

This example illustrates the basic concept of WOCR, but for detailed configuration steps, it's advisable to consult additional resources on SAP SCN or other relevant blogs.

Queue:

Now, let's discuss the next terminology: Queue. A queue is essential for prioritizing work and determining which group of people will perform specific tasks. For example, certain tasks may be handled by a forklift, while others may be done manually. To make these decisions, a queue is necessary and is associated with a warehouse order.
Once we have the warehouse order, it should be assigned to a queue for further processing. How does this assignment happen? It is done through a queue determination table. Let's look at how it works.

From the above screen, if my warehouse order involves picking and the bin access type of the source storage bin is designated as Z002, then the warehouse order will be assigned the queue "ZOBD-HEAVY."

For example, consider the following warehouse order (in below screenshot), which is assigned to the queue "ZOBD-HEAVY." This order includes two warehouse tasks, both associated with the same warehouse order. The warehouse number is LEWH, the activity is 'PICK,' and the bin access type assigned in the bin master is "Z002," which corresponds to forklift access.

As a result, the system checks the queue determination table and assigns the appropriate queue to the warehouse order.

Bin master screen,

Now that we understand how to manage the queue, the next step is resource management.
In the warehouse, there are various resources, such as warehouse operators and forklifts, which are also considered resources. Each person operating a forklift will be classified under the forklift resource group. In this way, every resource will be assigned to its respective resource group.

So now we can understood how WO getting the queue on that, next process is resource management.

Resource management:

Resource and resource group we will create in the configuration.

The resource group must be configured as a prerequisite for this assignment. After that, this resource group will be assigned to the queue.

The resource group and queue have been assigned. Whenever this resource is accessed from the handheld device (RF device), the user can retrieve the warehouse orders assigned to that queue by simply inputting the queue name.

Conclusion:

By integrating these components, SAP EWM transforms outbound delivery into a highly efficient, automated process. This system reduces picking times, optimizes resource utilization, and improves overall warehouse productivity. The blog emphasizes that while these concepts may seem complex at first, understanding their interrelationships is crucial for effectively managing modern warehouse operations in SAP EWM.

Best Regards,

Kannan Chokkanathan

entry.title: February's Developer Challenge in SAP Community - Integration 🧩
entry.content:
The second SAP Developer Challenge of the year is now available! The Developer Advocates have created the SAP Community Escape House - a virtual escape house (consisting of multiple rooms) that you can play in your browser. Read on for all the details.

SAP Developer Challenges are all about having fun, trying new things, and learning along the way. You can also earn a badge in the SAP Community upon successful completion. For this year, all SAP Developer Challenges will happen in the SAP Community Escape House. For each challenge, we will unlock a new room in the house, and they all have a specific focus topic. Completing a challenge means successfully escaping the room by solving a series of coding-related tasks/questions. This month is all about Integration, specifically the optional hybrid integration runtime offered as part of SAP Integration Suite.

The details of January's challenge (UI5) are here: link.

How to participate

Visit the SAP Community Escape House and enter your numeric SAP Community user ID. If you don't know your user ID, go to your SAP Community profile. You can find the numeric ID at the end of the URL.

SAP Community Escape House - Enter your SAP Community ID


Once you see the SAP Community Escape House, select the Integration room and start exploring it.

SAP Community Escape House - Available rooms

The user interface of an escape room includes the room itself as well as a terminal. You can click the objects in the room to receive tips. Like in a real (physical) escape room, you will have to try out a few things and explore the room until you receive helpful tips and, eventually, instructions. These tips and instructions are printed in the terminal next to the room. The terminal is also used to submit solutions to tasks. Read the instructions you receive carefully to understand how.

SAP Community Escape House - Integration room

If you successfully escape the room (you will know when that happens), you will receive a badge in the SAP Community. We will assign the badges once the challenge closes.

The schedule

The integration room was unlocked on February 3rd and will stay unlocked until the end of the year. However, the challenge closes on February 28th, so you will have to escape the room until then to qualify for a badge. Unlike the previous year, this SAP Developer Challenge does not consist of weekly sub-challenges. The next room (next challenge) will be unlocked in March.

Good luck and happy escaping!

entry.title: Re: Issue getting DMS Folder Content in SAP Build Process...
entry.content:
Hi Dan,

The destination url does not have 'root' in it, this is how it looks

https://<host>/browser/repoID

Yes, when I said folderID - it is the object ID which we get once we upload a file via the SPA forms. Example of it - 

spa-res:cmis:folderid:PiZaWRq8AYvDLC83fQWAbOBCcUtpiqDZ-2P4D6oeprI

So in the folderPath property of "Get DMS Folder Content" - I tried the following options

1) /root/PiZaWRq8AYvDLC83fQWAbOBCcUtpiqDZ-2P4D6oeprI

2) root/PiZaWRq8AYvDLC83fQWAbOBCcUtpiqDZ-2P4D6oeprI

neither worked, but this time I get a different error - 404 not found

 

 

Regards

Sandip

entry.title: Re: AUTH - Allow Planners to change dimension attributes...

entry.title: Re: Issue getting DMS Folder Content in SAP Build Process...
entry.content:
You can try knocking a square peg in a round whole but it won't work. Not your fault: Some of the DMS activities need a folder and some need an objectID.

This needs a folder.

Until they add the option for the objectID, you will have to convert the objectID to a folder, using the Get Properties API.

Go into the store and create a project from the template "How to Use Document Management Service", which provides the "old" way of doing DMS calls. There is an automation called "Get folder content", which has 2 activities -- custom script and call web service with destination. This shows you how to call DMS by creating your own call. Modify the call according to the API ... it should be quite simple.

Here is the API for Get Properties: https://api.sap.com/api/GetPropertiesApi/path/get_browser__repository_id__root

Not that you need it here, but you only need he name so you could add the query parameter filter=cmis:name to return only the name.

entry.title: PR is getting changed
entry.content:
It has been observed that the PR is getting changed even if PR is released, User can edit the text in the Header or can delete the PR even if the PR is in release mode & even after PO made.

We need to create new role for below requirement. Kindly help to solve this PR/PO release , how we can restrict users for this.

  1. If Any PR  (Purchase Requisition) gets released at all 3 level, it should not be edited by first 2 approvers unless the third approver unreleases it.
  2. If PO (Purchase Order) is made using the same PR, none of all 3 PR approvers should make changes in it, whose PO is made.
  3. If PO is released at all 3 level, then also the same PR should not be edited by anyone.

entry.title: Re: Intelligent Invoice Scanning
entry.content:
Hi Kathy,

our solution will always scan header and item data and currently we do not have any configuration to let the system only scan header fields. Therefore you either can manually delete the items in the review step. Mass selection and deletion of items is supported there. In addition the underlying staging object in the review screen is OData-enabled, so you might try to delete the items that way.

Best regards,

Stefan

entry.title: Re: Site transports using WBTIMEX and merchandise categories
entry.content:
Just answering for the record.

WBTIMEX is used in SAP Retail to transport sites (stores), between DEV, QAS and PRD systems. We used, it will transport the sites and their customer/vendor info, which as it's master data won't travel if you use the SE01.

You can get an inconsistent data error in the WBTIMEX. For me it was that the site I was trying to transport had merchansise categories that existed in DEV but no in QAS. Editing the site with WB02 and removing the categories solved the problem and let me transport the site.

entry.title: Re: Introducing SAP CPQ Product Rest API available
entry.content:
@yoganandaThe translation node is already working.
Unfortunately, however, the dictionary names are still used as the key for the translations.
The translation node can be structured as follows to send translations for Name, Description, LongDescription and DescriptionBuilder, as well as attribute translations.

"translations": {
            "name": {
                "German": "name german translation"
            },
            "description": {
                "German": "description german description"
            },
            "longDescription": {},
            "descriptionBuilder": {},
            "attributes": []
        }

The new Product Integration API is not yet listed in the Business Accelerator Hub.
Has anyone already built the JSON Scheme or received it from SAP?

entry.title: SAP HR LSMW
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

Please, need help with LSMW. I have a business process where I have vacation record (PA2001-0107) and I need to add sick leave record (PA2001-0101), both records through PA30. Using PA30 we are receiving warning messages that this two records collide, afterwards operator just edits vacation record (as per business process).

I have requirement to wright LSMW form sick leave records, that will be generated as txt files form another system. For LSMW I've used batch input recording method and for execution - Display errors only, recordings are done correctly, but we don't see collision system messages, showing us that there is already vacation record, and this 2 records collide.

I don't want to change configuration for PA2001-0101 and PA2001-0107, but need to see warning message for those records that collide, to be able to change vacation (first record) afterwards through PA30. And I don't want either to execute LSMW using Process/Foreground. Is there any possibility to see warning messages in LSMW that we see in PA30? 

Thanks a lot for your help.

Regards,

Tamuna

entry.title: New Destinations UI available now in BETA version
entry.content:
A new Destinations UI for managing destination configuration objects is now available as a BETA version in the SAP BTP cockpit, side-by-side with the current UI. It is not yet at feature parity with the current UI, but feel free to try it out and send us your feedback.

Old Destination UI

Getting Started with the New Destinations UI

To start using the new Destinations UI, follow these steps:

  1. Access the SAP BTP Cockpit: Log in to your SAP BTP account and navigate to the cockpit.
  2. Navigate to Destinations: In the cockpit, go to the Connectivity section and select Destinations.
  3. Explore the New UI: you can access to the BETA version, you will see the new interface. Take some time to explore the different features and options available.
  4. Create a New Destination: Click on the "Create Destination" button and fill in the required details, such as the destination name, URL, and authentication method.
  5. Save and Test: Save your new destination and test the connection to ensure everything is configured correctly



Check Connection


Export Destination in (JSON, YAML & Properties)

Benefits of the New Destinations UI

The new Destinations UI offers several benefits for SAP BTP users:

  • Efficiency: The streamlined workflow and enhanced configuration options save time and reduce complexity.
  • Usability: The intuitive interface makes it easier for users to manage their destinations, even if they are new to SAP BTP.
  • Flexibility: The ability to configure advanced settings and custom headers provides greater flexibility in connecting to remote systems.
  • Visibility: Improved visibility into destination details helps users quickly identify and address any issues, ensuring smooth operation of their applications

Conclusion

The new Destinations UI in the SAP BTP cockpit is a significant step forward in simplifying the management of destination configuration objects. As a BETA version, it offers a glimpse into the future of SAP BTP, where usability and efficiency are at the forefront. If you have access to the BETA version, be sure to explore the new features and provide feedback to help shape the final release.
